title: Luxury Showroom Material Harmonizer
description: You are a luxury showroom designer balancing material properties across a commercial refrigerator and a glam velvet sofa. Adjust metalness, roughness, color tint, emissive glow, and opacity across both objects while respecting a shared material energy budget. Use undo/redo to refine your choices and achieve the target aesthetic score.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap/CDN) that loads CommercialRefrigerator.glb on the left and GlamVelvetSofa.glb on the right in a showroom scene. Camera: perspective at (0,1.5,4) looking at origin. Lighting: one warm AreaLight above, one cool DirectionalLight from the side, ambient at 0.3 intensity. Add a reflective floor plane with MeshStandardMaterial (metalness 0.9, roughness 0.1). Core mechanic: implement a shared Material Energy Budget of 5.0 total units. Each object has five material channels: metalness (0-1), roughness (0-1), color tint RGB (each 0-1 costs 0.3 per channel), emissive intensity (0-1), and opacity (0.2-1.0). Every slider adjustment on one object's material consumes budget; if budget is exceeded, the other object's corresponding channel is automatically reduced to compensate (conservation law). Build an HTML panel with two columns of sliders (one per object) for all five properties. Display the remaining energy budget as a progress bar. Implement a coupling toggle: when enabled, adjusting roughness on the refrigerator inversely adjusts metalness on the sofa and vice versa (multi-input coupling). Add undo/redo buttons that track a history stack of all material state changes (minimum 20 states). Apply material changes in real-time by traversing each loaded model's meshes and updating their MeshStandardMaterial properties. Add a target aesthetic: display a small reference swatch showing target metalness/roughness/color for each object. Compute a match score (0-100%) using Euclidean distance across all channels. Display the score live. The task is complete when score exceeds 90%. Use OrbitControls for camera. Ensure all slider interactions, budget enforcement, coupling, and undo/redo work together seamlessly. Each interaction step (slider drag, coupling toggle, undo, redo) counts toward the 10-14 step goal.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  refrigeratorMetalness: number,         // metalness value of refrigerator material (0-1)
  refrigeratorRoughness: number,         // roughness value of refrigerator material (0-1)
  refrigeratorEmissive: number,         // emissive intensity of refrigerator material (0-1)
  refrigeratorOpacity: number,         // opacity of refrigerator material (0.2-1.0)
  sofaMetalness: number,         // metalness value of sofa material (0-1)
  sofaRoughness: number,         // roughness value of sofa material (0-1)
  sofaEmissive: number,         // emissive intensity of sofa material (0-1)
  sofaOpacity: number,         // opacity of sofa material (0.2-1.0)
  energyBudgetRemaining: number,         // remaining material energy budget units (0-5.0)
  couplingEnabled: boolean,         // whether inverse metalness-roughness coupling is active
  historyDepth: number,         // count of undo/redo history entries available
  totalAdjustments: number,         // cumulative count of material property adjustments made
}
